name: supply_chain
system_prompt: You are a supply chain manager responsible for end-to-end logistics, procurement, and inventory optimization. Use the available tools to track shipments, forecast demand, manage suppliers, and optimize costs while maintaining service levels.

tools.name: shipment_tracker
tools.description: Track shipment status, location, ETA, and delivery history across carriers and modes

tools.name: demand_forecaster
tools.description: Generate demand forecasts using historical data, seasonality, and market signals

tools.name: supplier_database
tools.description: Look up supplier information, performance scorecards, certifications, and pricing

tools.name: route_optimizer
tools.description: Optimize shipping routes, carrier selection, and mode choices for cost and speed

tools.name: inventory_manager
tools.description: Check inventory levels, set reorder points, manage safety stock, and track turns

tools.name: procurement_system
tools.description: Create purchase orders, manage contracts, run RFQs, and approve spend

contrast_types.just_in_time_vs_safety_stock: One request minimizes inventory through JIT delivery, the other builds buffer stock for resilience. Example: "Set up daily deliveries to eliminate warehouse costs" vs "Increase safety stock to cover 30 days of demand"
contrast_types.single_source_vs_multi_source: One request relies on a single preferred supplier, the other diversifies across multiple suppliers. Example: "Negotiate a volume discount with our primary supplier" vs "Qualify two backup suppliers for critical components"
contrast_types.cost_vs_speed_optimization: One request optimizes for lowest cost (slower modes, consolidation), the other for fastest delivery. Example: "Ship via ocean freight to save costs" vs "Air freight these parts, production is waiting"
contrast_types.local_vs_global_sourcing: One request sources from local/regional suppliers, the other from international/global suppliers. Example: "Find a domestic supplier for this material" vs "Source this from the cheapest global supplier"
contrast_types.demand_pull_vs_supply_push: One request responds to actual customer demand (pull), the other proactively pushes inventory based on forecasts. Example: "Replenish only what customers ordered" vs "Pre-position inventory based on the holiday forecast"
contrast_types.spot_buy_vs_contract: One request makes a one-time spot purchase, the other negotiates a long-term contract. Example: "Buy 500 units at the best available price today" vs "Negotiate a 12-month blanket PO with volume tiers"
